everyone.name: Priyanka Saggu
everyone.bio: Priyanka Saggu is a Kubernetes Integration Engineer at SUSE. She has made significant contributions to the Kubernetes project via Special Interest Groups – Release, ContribEx, Testing and CLI.  She's the Release Lead for Kubernetes v1.29 release cycle, and is the Emeritus Advisor for the upcoming Kubernetes v1.31 release cycle.  Priyanka is also the Technical Lead for the Kubernetes SIG - Contributor Experience (ContribEx) and a GitHub Admin for the Kubernetes project's GitHub organizations.  In the past, she has held leadership roles such as Release Lead Shadow for v1.27 and v1.26, Enhancement Lead for v1.25, and has been part of the Release Team since v1.23.  She actively mentors new contributors and coordinates the Meet Our Contributor - APAC initiative under SIG ContribEx to promote the achievements of active contributors.

everyone.name: Anita Ihuman
everyone.bio: Anita Ihuman is a developer advocate and community strategist with over 6+ years of experience driving contributions and growth of cloud-native tech and developer communities. Anita is a cloud evangelist promoting the growth and adoption of platform and cloud-native solutions across Africa, serving as an organizer for Kubernetes Community Days (KCD) Nigeria and the CNCF Abuja community group. She serves as a maintainer and Governing Board member at CHAOSS. She also contributes as an advisory board member at OpenUK, a member of the NumFOCUS DISC Committee, and proudly contributes to the DevNetwork Cloud Advisory Board—sharing insights that help shape the future of cloud technology.

everyone.name: Koray Oksay
everyone.bio: He works at Kubermatic as a Kubernetes Consultant and Trainer to help companies with their cloud-native journey. Before that, he worked for startup and enterprise companies in the advertising, banking, and telecom industries as SysAdmin, Application Admin, DevOps Engineer, and SRE for more than 20 years.

everyone.name: Vera
everyone.bio: A sunny person ☀️ In love with the cloud

everyone.name: Vivekananda Kadavergu
everyone.bio: I’m Vivek, a platform engineering and developer experience practitioner with more than a decade designing and building complex systems. Domain expertise: payments, card acquiring/issuing, and KYC. I’ve built internal developer platforms and centralized DevOps CI/CD pipelines, plus API-first, event-driven/microservice frameworks that reduce cross-cutting concerns and improve delivery consistency. I work across Kafka, Kubernetes, service mesh, cloud, and automation, and I lead engineering teams end-to-end with a focus on security and operational rigor. Outside work: travelling and chess.

everyone.name: Balaji Ravi
everyone.bio: With over 13+ years of IT experience, I am a DevOps professional with a solid track record in the Engineering services and Insurance sectors. I am adept at developing robust infrastructure solutions and enhancing software delivery pipelines. My expertise spans a broad range of state-of-the-art technologies, including cloud platforms (AWS, Azure), containerization and orchestration (Kubernetes, Docker), infrastructure as code (Terraform), CI/CD tools (GitLab CI/CD, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps), configuration management (Ansible), as well as monitoring and logging. I am proficient in code quality tools (SonarQube), version control systems (Git, GitHub, Bitbucket), automation scripting (Linux Shell Scripting), service mesh (Istio), and package management (Helm). I specialize in designing scalable, robust, and secure cloud-native solutions, and I am dedicated to advancing automation to optimize development processes

ambassadors.name: Priyanka Saggu
ambassadors.bio: Priyanka Saggu is a Kubernetes Integration Engineer at SUSE. She has made significant contributions to the Kubernetes project via Special Interest Groups – Release, ContribEx, Testing and CLI.  She's the Release Lead for Kubernetes v1.29 release cycle, and is the Emeritus Advisor for the upcoming Kubernetes v1.31 release cycle.  Priyanka is also the Technical Lead for the Kubernetes SIG - Contributor Experience (ContribEx) and a GitHub Admin for the Kubernetes project's GitHub organizations.  In the past, she has held leadership roles such as Release Lead Shadow for v1.27 and v1.26, Enhancement Lead for v1.25, and has been part of the Release Team since v1.23.  She actively mentors new contributors and coordinates the Meet Our Contributor - APAC initiative under SIG ContribEx to promote the achievements of active contributors.

ambassadors.name: Christina Andonov
ambassadors.bio: I am a Solutions Architect at AWS where I help organization adopt Kubernetes at scale. The two areas I focus on are Platform Engineering and Data on Kubernetes.

ambassadors.name: Thomas Labarussias
ambassadors.bio: I'm OSS/Ecosystem Advocate at Sysdig, the company which created and open-sourced Falco, the Security Runtime Engine for Kubernetes and Cloud-Native technologies. I worked for Qonto, a modern banking for SMEs and freelancers, where I managed their Kubernetes clusters and the enthusiastic tools around, like ArgoCD, Traefik, Prometheus. I also assisted for many years pure-players and e-business companies for a large managed service provider, as an AWS expert and FinOps. I'm one of the longest tenured members of the Falco community, and the creator of Falcosidekick and Falcosidekick-UI, two major components of the Falco ecosystem.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: As an SRE for several years, I've been able to use a number of CNCF projects in production. I'm also one of the maintainers of the most advanced OSS security project in the landscape. I would like to help even more architects and users to integrate these projects into their infrastructures. Moreover, in 2024, the KubeCon EU will be in Paris, France, it's important for me to do my best to help the organizers and show the vitality of French tech.

ambassadors.name: Paul Balogh
ambassadors.bio: Paul is a Software Architect and Developer Advocate. In his many roles, he has encouraged developers and testers alike to "shift left," bringing reliability testing earlier in the software development process. As a software engineer/architect for over 20 years in varying languages, he now focuses more on the Go programming language. Active in the community, he organizes the St. Louis chapter of Cloud Native Community Groups and the StLGo Meetup group.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: As a technologist, I became enamored with cloud-native technologies as soon as I was exposed. Events like KubeCon only supercharged my excitement for the overall CNCF project landscape. As a CNCF Ambassador, I'm hoping my enthusiasm for all things cloud-native will spread to those I meet at local events. I feel this platform will allow me to become more engaged as an advocate for CNCF projects, helping the community to gain more knowledge of opportunities and to encourage more active roles in open source contributions.

ambassadors.name: Whitney Lee
ambassadors.bio: Whitney is a kind and curious human who loves exploring and explaining tools in the cloud native landscape. She has delivered two KubeCon keynotes and countless fun, funny, and informative community talks. She runs a vibrant YouTube channel, co-hosts You Choose!—a ‘Choose-Your-Own-Adventure’-style journey through the CNCF landscape—and is also a host of Cloud Native Live and the podcast Software Defined Interviews.

ambassadors.name: Bmk Lakshminarayanan
ambassadors.bio: Transformation Architect, SECTION6 BMK is an inspiring and passionate DevOps Advocate and Value Stream Architect promoting DevOps, Lean and Value Stream Management principles & practices. He is also a New Zealand ambassador for the Cloud-Native Computing Foundation(CNCF), DevOps Institute and a Board Advisor for the Value Stream Management consortium(VSMC). He hosts the CNCF meetup in Wellington, New Zealand and is co-chair of Cloud-Native Summit A/NZ. He blogs about his experiences and learnings at https://bmk.nz/Why I am excited to be an ambassador: 1. Promoting Open Source and Cloud-Native projects and ecosystem tools in our community. 2. Connecting with experts, maintainers, contributors and other Opensource and cloud-native thought leaders to amplify my learning 3. Use the ambassador program and help in bringing in the best to my community in terms of cloud-native learnings, education and training.

ambassadors.name: Maxime Coquerel
ambassadors.bio: Maxime Coquerel is a Principal Cloud Security Architect driving cloud threat research, cloud security architecture, threat model and risk assessments across enterprise-scale designs. Passionate about knowledge sharing, he collaborates with cloud vendors and emerging startups to co-develop innovative cloud security solutions, while actively contributing to the global cloud-native community through mentoring, blogging, and community events to inspire secure and scalable adoption of CNCF technologies.

ambassadors.name: Jesse Meng
ambassadors.bio: I am a cloud native believer and advocator with over 15 years of experience in cloud computing. I was an early contributor to Kubernetes and led its adoption at eBay for six years. I am currently working as a product and development owner of cloud native FinOps at Tencent Cloud, where I founded the open-source project Crane, which helps users optimize their cloud costs. I am also auther of "The Journey of Kubernetes Production Adoption Success", translator of "Cloud FinOps", and speaker on various cloud native topics including Cloud Native Campus in geektime.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am excited to be an ambassador because I love cloud native and I want to share my passion and knowledge with others. I think cloud native is the future of software development and it can bring many benefits to users, developers, and businesses. I want to help the cloud native community grow and thrive by contributing to open-source projects, writing books and articles, and organizing events. Being an ambassador will give me more opportunities to connect with other cloud native enthusiasts and learn from their experiences. I hope to inspire more people to join the cloud native journey and make a positive impact on the world.

kubestronauts.name: Vera
kubestronauts.bio: A sunny person ☀️ In love with the cloud

kubestronauts.name: Erwin Espejo
kubestronauts.bio: I am a result-driven and experienced Network, Cloud, MicroServices and Security Consultant with extensive experience in the engineering, cybersecurity, consulting, architecture, administration, pre-sales and post-sales consultant of various advanced technologies. Years of expertise in safeguarding organisational networks, systems, cloud and sensitive data from evolving cyber threats.

kubestronauts.name: Jakub Hovorka
kubestronauts.bio: I'm a DevOps Engineer from the Netherlands passionate about Kubernetes and GitOps. My favorite tools within the CNCF landscape are ArgoCD, Helm, and Prometheus.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Eliezer Weisman
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I have been gaining experience with Kubernetes over the past year and am currently expanding my knowledge. My goal is to achieve the Golden Kubestronaut and delve deeper into the cloud-native ecosystem. I am excited to continue learning and look forward to connecting with the community.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Alex Au
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I am a Hong Kong–based cloud platform engineer specializing in Cloud-Native technologies and AI/ML systems. I have been focusing on building AI Cloud Native Infrastructures as well as Enterprise Platforms in the past few years. Apart from work, I am active in the open-source community, co-organizing Hong Kong Python User Group meetups and has been a speaker at events like PyCon Hong Kong 2023 and 2024 to share Cloud-Native best practices.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Hengzhou Wu
goldenKubestronauts.bio: A Cloud Infrastructure Engineer based on AWS and GCP. Provides cloud technical support and solutions, specializing in cloud cost optimization and monitoring. In my spare time, I enjoy learning about techniques and articles related to containers, Kubernetes, and Prometheus.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Gerard Van Engelen
goldenKubestronauts.bio: As a seasoned DevOps engineer, I pride myself on ensuring the quality of products by drawing parallels between complex issues and simpler, everyday scenarios. I specialize in observability mainly LGTM stack.
